evidence: Direct foundation: closed-form Taylor + ghost-technique.
Primary head-to-head baseline; Fairds replaces bi-level meta-learning with closed-form Shapley.
Static Shapley-for-fairness predecessor; Fairds is the dynamic in-run analogue.
Validation-set-driven reweighting precursor (1-step bi-level).
Shares no-demographics goal — Fairds achieves it via balanced D_val anchor.
Contemporaneous single-training-run Shapley variant (accuracy-focused; Fairds is fairness-focused).
Repurposes In-Run Shapley as in-run reweighting controller for fairness.
Closed-form Shapley provides meta-fairness via 4 axioms; FORML weights have no such guarantee.
Validation utility is plain loss — no sensitive-attribute labels required on D_val.
Inherits In-Run Shapley's GPT-2 / Pythia-410M validation, enabling foundation-model-scale extension.
Repurposes 2nd-order gradient-Hessian-gradient cross-term as a representation-bias attenuator (the most novel hypothesis).
E1 (toy 2-group LR) is the controlled testbed for the auto-balancing claim.
E2 head-to-head on Adult/COMPAS measures DP-diff and EO-diff vs FORML.
E2 includes the sensitive-label-on/off ablation on D_val.
E3 extends C2 to vision-scale (CelebA, ResNet-50).
E4 measures wall-clock overhead across Vanilla/Fairds-1st/2nd/FORML.
E5 sweeps |D_val| and group composition; quantifies sensitive-label-free regime.
E5 is the primary D_val sensitivity sweep.
E6 measures Symmetry/Efficiency/Dummy axiom violations on Fairds and FORML.
E7 lifts C2 to LM scale (GPT-2 small + BBQ/BOLD).
Mann-Whitney U(maj<min) p=1 at ratio=0.9, Δφ monotone increasing across {0.5,0.7,0.9,0.99}, alpha sweep insufficient.
Naive per-sample gradient: 11-30× vanilla wall-clock, far above 1.5×/3.0× target. Vectorization deferred to E2/E4.
Mann-Whitney U(maj<min) p=1.89e-08 at ratio=0.9; Δφ monotone decreasing across ratios; isolation gain over fairds-1 +0.7-1.3pp worst-acc.
Ren2018 (FORML prototype) achieves better fairness with worse accuracy on Adult/COMPAS; Fairds-2 trade-off favors accuracy but cannot match fairness improvement.
Random-anchor fairness recovery drops to 30-60% of balanced-anchor recovery on Adult, even worse on COMPAS.
fairds-2 vs fairds-1 isolation: Δtest_worst=+4.7pp p=0.033 on held-out OOD; Δval_eval_worst=+3.6pp p=0.012.
fairds-2 vs corrected Ren2018: Δtest_worst=+2.7pp p=0.052 marginal; weakly supports head-to-head claim on OOD spurious benchmark.
Three Waterbirds variants (default, warmup_epochs=3, no_ema per-step) all fail to approach corrected Ren2018 (Δ-29 to -35pp test_worst). Confines C2 support to from-scratch / small-model regimes.
Realistic-scale measurement: 4.3-7.7x vs target 1.5-3x. Fairds-1 faster than corrected Ren2018 baseline (4.3x vs 4.78x); Fairds-2 slower (7.68x vs 4.78x).
